Caption: The structure of the intelligent control framework with Rule-Based Large Language Model Decision-Making.

The UAV’s actual trajectory compared to the reference trajectory during a figure-eight maneuver, with the RB-LLM-based adaptation mechanism activated. The reasoning-based control layer effectively compensates for a 15% mass parameter error, enabling accurate trajectory tracking despite the presence of modeling uncertainty.

Position trajectories along the x, y, and z axes compared to their respective reference trajectories under the RB-LLM-based adaptation framework. The LLM-driven control adjustments facilitate precise tracking of the desired trajectories across all spatial dimensions.

UAV trajectory and the reference trajectory without RB-LLM adaptation. In the absence of the reasoning-based adaptation layer, the multirotor exhibits larger deviations from the desired trajectory due to uncompensated mass errors, highlighting the limitations of the nominal controller under parametric uncertainty.

Time-stamped 3D trajectory comparison between the desired trajectory and the actual UAV trajectory with RB-LLM adaptation enabled. The simulation introduces a model mismatch by increasing the payload by 0.21 kg. Despite this unmodeled disturbance, the RB-LLM framework effectively minimizes trajectory tracking errors across all three spatial axes, demonstrating its robust adaptive capability.

Position tracking errors along the x, y, and z axes. Errors quickly converge to near-zero after initial transients, highlighting the RB-LLM controller’s fast adaptation and stability under model uncertainty.

3D trajectory of the UAV without RB-LLM adaptation under a 0.21 kg payload disturbance. The deviation from the reference trajectory highlights the effects of model mismatch and the need for real-time adaptive control.

3D UAV trajectory under severe disturbance with RB-LLM adaptation. Following a 15% mass reduction and a high-frequency external oscillation introduced at t = 20 s , the RB-LLM framework adaptively adjusts control inputs in real time and initiates an emergency landing upon detecting.

3D UAV trajectory without RB-LLM adaptation during disturbance. Without high-level reasoning, the controller fails to compensate for the 15% mass reduction and oscillatory disturbance introduced at t = 20 s , resulting in significant trajectory deviation, degraded performance, and loss of flightstability.
